name: Taj Mahal
summary: The Taj Mahal is an ivory-white marble mausoleum on the right bank of the river Yamuna in Agra, India. Commissioned in 1632 by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan to house the tomb of his favorite wife, Mumtaz Mahal, it is widely regarded as one of the most beautiful buildings in the world and a masterpiece of Mughal architecture.

timeline.title: Construction Begins
timeline.description: Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan commissioned the Taj Mahal after the death of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal during childbirth. Construction began with the foundation and the main tomb structure, employing over 20,000 artisans from across the empire.

timeline.title: Main Structure Completed
timeline.description: The main mausoleum structure was completed after 11 years of intensive construction. The iconic white marble dome and the four minarets were finished, showcasing the pinnacle of Mughal architectural achievement.

timeline.title: Full Complex Completed
timeline.description: The entire Taj Mahal complex was completed after 22 years of construction, including the gardens, guest house, mosque, and the main gateway. The total cost was estimated at 32 million rupees at the time.

timeline.title: British Colonial Era
timeline.description: During the Indian Rebellion of 1857, the Taj Mahal suffered some damage and was used as a garrison. The British colonial government later undertook restoration efforts to preserve the monument.

timeline.title: British Restoration
timeline.description: Lord Curzon, the Viceroy of India, ordered a major restoration of the Taj Mahal, replacing stolen precious stones and restoring the gardens to their original Mughal charbagh (four-garden) layout.

timeline.title: UNESCO World Heritage Site
timeline.description: The Taj Mahal was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized as 'the jewel of Muslim art in India and one of the universally admired masterpieces of the world's heritage.'
